summary: Justin Brennan coaches Brian Norton's AIOS multifamily students on broker relationship-building and deal flow strategy. The session covers how to identify market-maker brokers by analyzing listing logos on Crexy, making cold calls, following up persistently, and conducting in-person market trips to warm relationships. While directly applicable to Brian's deal-sourcing challenge, the session also covers capital-raising foundations and mock deals.
takeaways: Identify market-maker brokers by frequency of logos on Crexy—brokers with most listings have 3+ off-market deals behind every 1 public listing
Call brokers on specific Crexy listings as conversation lure, then pivot to asking 'Do you have anything else you're working on behind the scenes?'
Send LOI immediately, then call/text broker same day saying 'It's aggressive but I'd like to go over numbers to see where I may be wrong'
Follow up with brokers once per week; don't blast 100, cultivate 3-4 quality relationships in each market
Make market trip 1-2 weeks after initial broker call, text ahead: 'We connected earlier, coming to town, love to grab coffee and put face to name'
Pre-market deals are the real opportunity—Crexy/Loopnet deals 'suck' so broker relationships give you access to deals before they list publicly
Brokers are cold because they receive many wishy-washy calls; you must be the 'hunter' not 'gatherer'—'closed mouths don't get fed'

skill_candidates.change: Shift from random cold outreach to targeted broker identification using listing frequency analysis
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin shows filtering Crexy by logos to find 'market makers' and explains 'For every single M you see publicly listed, they have 3 behind the scenes guaranteed'

skill_candidates.change: Use specific deal discussions as conversation opener before pivoting to relationship building
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin calls on a specific Crexy listing and says 'I don't care about this deal. It's a lure. I care about you and the relationship with you'

skill_candidates.change: Implement same-day follow-up protocol after submitting LOI with specific pre-framing language
skill_candidates.evidence: 'I follow up the second I send it...saying Hey John just sent you an LOI...It's a bit aggressive, but I'd like to go over numbers to see where I may be wrong'

skill_candidates.change: Prioritize in-person market trips as relationship-closing mechanism after initial phone rapport
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin notes he didn't get serious until after KC market trip, then 'within 30 days locked up first deal' because brokers felt comfortable due to face-to-face trust-building

title: Weekly Coaching_04-09-26 | Justin Brennan
